# Project Starfall Code Organization Plan

Date: 2026-06-12

## Objective

Organize Project Starfall so the code is easier to read, edit, test, and extend while maintaining identical runtime behavior.

This is a behavior-preserving refactor plan. Visual output, save format, route behavior, game mechanics, asset paths, public globals, and test expectations should remain unchanged unless a later task explicitly approves a functional change.

## Current State

Project Starfall already has a recognizable vertical slice:

- Page shell: `pages/games/project-starfall.html`
- Data bundle: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-data.js`
- Character rig: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-rig.js`
- Pixi renderer: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-renderer-pixi.js`
- Runtime engine: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-engine.js`
- UI/controller layer: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-ui.js`
- Boot file: `js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-main.js`
- Styles: `css/games/project-starfall.css`
- Asset processors: `build/*project-starfall*.js`
- Runtime and balance coverage: `test.js`, `tests/project-starfall-balance-harness.js`

The macro-architecture is understandable, but the major files have grown into broad catch-all modules. The largest maintainability risks are:

- `project-starfall-engine.js` mixes state creation, map runtime, combat, loot, inventory, cards, Plinko, progression, renderer fallback drawing, performance telemetry, persistence, and asset loading.
- `project-starfall-ui.js` mixes snapshot management, keybinds, drag/drop, modal prompts, canvas windowing, hit regions, panel rendering, character select, storage, inventory, cards, Plinko, admin/debug UI, and settings.
- `project-starfall-data.js` combines all static domain data and derived asset registries in one large file.
- `project-starfall.css` contains all Starfall page, shell, modal, panel, inventory, character select, card, Plinko, and responsive styling in one file.
- Runtime assets, source sheets, and backup/provenance files are partially mixed under `img/project-starfall`.
- Some formatting drift exists in Starfall files, especially tab indentation in the largest JS/CSS files.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

- Preserve public script load order and browser globals:
  - `ProjectStarfallData`
  - `ProjectStarfallRig`
  - `ProjectStarfallPixiRenderer`
  - `ProjectStarfallEngine`
  - `createProjectStarfallEngine`
  - `ProjectStarfallUi`
  - `createProjectStarfallUi`
- Preserve `module.exports` compatibility used by `test.js` and the balance harness.
- Preserve localStorage keys and save/roster shape unless a migration is explicitly planned.
- Preserve asset paths referenced by data, CSS, build scripts, and tests.
- Preserve `/games/project-starfall` and `/project-starfall` route behavior.
- Keep each refactor slice small enough to validate with focused syntax checks and Starfall tests.
- Do not combine refactors with gameplay, balance, or visual changes.

## Recommended Target Shape

Keep the current no-framework browser architecture, but split by stable domains.

```text
js/games/project-starfall/
  project-starfall-main.js
  project-starfall-data.js              # temporary generated/compat bundle
  project-starfall-engine.js            # temporary facade during migration
  project-starfall-ui.js                # temporary facade during migration
  core/
    constants.js
    math.js
    ids.js
    time.js
    storage.js
  data/
    assets.js
    classes.js
    skills.js
    enemies.js
    maps.js
    items.js
    cards.js
    quests.js
    progression.js
    index.js
  engine/
    state.js
    engine.js
    assets.js
    viewport.js
    maps.js
    movement.js
    combat.js
    skills.js
    enemies.js
    loot.js
    inventory.js
    equipment.js
    cards.js
    plinko.js
    quests.js
    monster-guide.js
    party.js
    progression.js
    performance.js
    snapshots.js
    canvas-fallback-renderer.js
  ui/
    ui.js
    snapshots.js
    canvas-windows.js
    hit-regions.js
    input.js
    keybinds.js
    drag-drop.js
    prompts.js
    panels/
      character.js
      equipment.js
      inventory.js
      storage.js
      skills.js
      quests.js
      worldmap.js
      monsters.js
      shop.js
      upgrade.js
      plinko.js
      cards.js
      party.js
      pet.js
      settings.js
      admin.js
  rendering/
    rig.js                              # eventual home for current project-starfall-rig.js
    pixi-renderer.js                    # eventual home for current renderer
```

CSS should follow the same split:

```text
css/games/project-starfall/
  index.css
  shell.css
  loading.css
  character-select.css
  hud.css
  windows.css
  prompts.css
  inventory.css
  cards.css
  skills.css
  plinko.css
  admin.css
  responsive.css
```

`css/games/project-starfall.css` should stay as the compatibility import/entry file until the build pipeline and route style map are updated safely.

## Phased Plan

### Phase 0: Baseline And Guardrails

Goal: establish a known-good baseline before moving code.

Actions:

- Run focused checks and record current status:
  - `node --check js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-data.js`
  - `node --check js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-engine.js`
  - `node --check js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-ui.js`
  - `node --check js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-renderer-pixi.js`
  - `npm run test:starfall:smoke`
  - `npm run test:starfall:systems`
  - `npm run test:starfall:inventory`
- Capture the current public API surface:
  - exported globals
  - CommonJS exports
  - localStorage keys
  - script order in the page
  - Starfall route rewrites
- Add a short refactor checklist to future PRs or task notes:
  - no gameplay changes
  - no save shape changes
  - no asset path changes
  - focused tests run

Acceptance criteria:

- Current behavior is documented as the baseline.
- No source files are reorganized yet.

### Phase 1: Mechanical Formatting Cleanup

Goal: remove indentation drift that makes future diffs harder to review.

Actions:

- Normalize tab indentation to two-space indentation in Starfall JS/CSS only.
- Avoid any semantic edits.
- Use syntax checks and focused tests after formatting.
- Keep the diff isolated from extraction work.

Acceptance criteria:

- Starfall files use the repository's two-space convention.
- No behavior, selectors, exports, or data values change.

### Phase 2: Extract Pure Shared Utilities

Goal: move low-risk helper functions before touching stateful systems.

Candidate extractions:

- `core/math.js`: `clamp`, seeded random helpers, range helpers, rectangle overlap helpers.
- `core/time.js`: time/date helpers, daily login date helpers, `performance.now` fallback.
- `core/ids.js`: id normalization and lookup helpers.
- `core/storage.js`: safe JSON/localStorage helpers.

Approach:

- Extract functions with no side effects first.
- Keep the old global facade files exporting the same public API.
- If browser module loading is not introduced, concatenate or require these helpers inside the existing IIFE pattern.

Acceptance criteria:

- No public API changes.
- Existing tests pass.
- New helper files can be independently syntax checked.

### Phase 3: Split Static Data By Domain

Goal: make data edits safer without changing runtime data shape.

Actions:

- Create domain data files for assets, classes, skills, enemies, maps, items, cards, quests, progression, and economy.
- Add a data index that assembles the exact current `ProjectStarfallData` object.
- Keep `project-starfall-data.js` as the browser/CommonJS compatibility bundle until script loading changes are safe.
- Preserve all object keys and array ordering.

Acceptance criteria:

- `require('./js/games/project-starfall/project-starfall-data.js')` returns the same shape as before.
- Asset path tests still pass.
- Balance harness continues to consume the same data object.

### Phase 4: Extract Engine Systems

Goal: reduce `ProjectStarfallEngine` into an orchestrator with domain modules.

Suggested extraction order:

1. Asset loading and asset preview catalog.
2. Viewport/camera/canvas sizing.
3. Inventory and storage-independent item helpers.
4. Equipment, stat breakdowns, potentials, and upgrades.
5. Cards and card decks.
6. Plinko runtime and rewards.
7. Combat metrics and performance telemetry.
8. Loot/drop tables and target farming.
9. Map runtime, spawn sections, portals, and mechanics.
10. Combat, skills, enemies, party, and pet behavior.
11. Canvas 2D fallback renderer.
12. Snapshot creation and UI change patching.

Approach:

- Extract one domain at a time.
- Prefer pure functions that accept `(state, data, runtime, options)` before class-heavy abstractions.
- Leave thin methods on `ProjectStarfallEngine` when tests or UI call them directly.
- Treat the engine class as the compatibility surface until all callers are migrated.

Acceptance criteria:

- Existing `createProjectStarfallEngine(null, data)` tests still work.
- UI callers do not need broad changes during each extraction.
- Runtime Starfall test suites pass after each domain slice.

### Phase 5: Extract UI Panels And Input Systems

Goal: reduce `ProjectStarfallUi` into a coordinator for snapshots, input, windows, and panels.

Suggested extraction order:

1. Snapshot refresh/cache helpers.
2. Keybind definitions and assignment helpers.
3. Canvas hit-region lookup and hover/wheel routing.
4. Shared canvas window geometry and prompt helpers.
5. Inventory drag/drop and context menu helpers.
6. Panels with the clearest boundaries:
   - Settings
   - Admin
   - Skills
   - Character
   - Equipment
   - Inventory
   - Storage
   - Monster Guide
   - Quests
   - World Map
   - Shop
   - Upgrade
   - Plinko
   - Cards
   - Party/Pet

Approach:

- Keep `ProjectStarfallUi` as the event and lifecycle owner.
- Extract panel renderers as functions or small objects that receive a context object:
  - `snapshot`
  - `engine`
  - formatting helpers
  - action/hit-region registration callbacks
- Avoid moving multiple panels in one slice.

Acceptance criteria:

- Canvas actions and keyboard shortcuts behave the same.
- Existing string-contract tests either continue passing or are updated to reference the new module while preserving the same behavior.
- Visual verification is run for any panel whose renderer is moved.

### Phase 6: Split CSS

Goal: make UI styling changes local to the surface being edited.

Actions:

- Create `css/games/project-starfall/` partials.
- Move CSS in groups matching UI domains.
- Keep `css/games/project-starfall.css` as the entry file that imports the partials.
- Keep selectors and cascade order stable during the first split.

Suggested order:

1. Shell/stage/loading/start screen.
2. Character select.
3. HUD and meters.
4. Modal/window base.
5. Inventory/equipment/cards.
6. Skills/quests/monster guide/world map.
7. Shop/upgrade/Plinko.
8. Admin/settings/responsive rules.

Acceptance criteria:

- Route style map still references `css/games/project-starfall.css`.
- Built CSS contains the same selectors.
- Screenshots or manual browser checks confirm no visible regression.

### Phase 7: Asset Tree Cleanup

Goal: separate deployable runtime assets from source and backup material.

Actions:

- Define canonical source locations:
  - `asset-sources/project-starfall/` for source sheets and generated-image inputs.
  - `img/project-starfall/` for runtime deployable assets only.
  - optional `docs/project-starfall-assets/` for provenance notes if needed.
- Move source/backups only after updating processors and tests.
- Keep `build/copy-to-public.js` exclusions aligned with the new tree.
- Keep runtime `ProjectStarfallData` asset paths unchanged unless all consumers and tests are updated in the same slice.

Acceptance criteria:

- `npm run build:project-starfall-assets` still works.
- Runtime asset tests pass.
- Public output excludes source/backups.

### Phase 8: Build And Test Refinement

Goal: make the new organization easy to validate.

Actions:

- Add focused test commands if needed for extracted domains.
- Consider moving Starfall string-contract tests out of `test.js` into Starfall-specific test modules.
- Keep `test.js` as the top-level runner if that fits the repo's dependency-free style.
- Add lightweight module-level tests for pure helpers as they are extracted.

Acceptance criteria:

- Developers can run narrow checks while working on one Starfall domain.
- Full Starfall gates remain available:
  - `npm run test:starfall:smoke`
  - `npm run test:starfall:systems`
  - `npm run test:starfall:combat`
  - `npm run test:starfall:inventory`
  - `npm run test:starfall:balance`
  - `npm run test:starfall:full`

## Work Slice Template

Each refactor slice should follow this pattern:

1. Pick one narrow domain.
2. Record the current public methods, selectors, data keys, and tests that touch it.
3. Move code with compatibility shims.
4. Run syntax checks.
5. Run the smallest relevant Starfall test suite.
6. If UI changed location, run a browser smoke check for `/games/project-starfall`.
7. Review diff for accidental behavior changes.

## Suggested First Three Implementation Slices

### Slice 1: Formatting And Refactor Guardrails

- Normalize tab indentation in Starfall JS/CSS.
- Add a short Starfall refactor checklist to this document if new guardrails are discovered.
- Run syntax checks and `npm run test:starfall:smoke`.

### Slice 2: Extract Core Utility Helpers

- Create `core/math.js`, `core/time.js`, and `core/ids.js`.
- Move pure helpers used by both engine and UI.
- Keep compatibility through the existing IIFE/CommonJS pattern.
- Run syntax checks and `npm run test:starfall:systems`.

### Slice 3: Extract Asset Loading And Asset Catalog

- Move asset path collection, load progress, preload, backup-path, and asset-preview catalog logic out of the engine.
- Keep engine methods as wrappers.
- Run asset-related Starfall tests and `npm run test:starfall:smoke`.

## Progress Log

### 2026-06-12: CSS Shell Partial Started

- Created `css/games/project-starfall/shell.css`.
- Moved the `.project-starfall-page` shell variables and page-level background/color declaration into the new partial.
- Kept `css/games/project-starfall.css` as the public compatibility entry file via `@import url("project-starfall/shell.css");`.
- This is intentionally the smallest CSS split: route manifests, page links, tests, and public stylesheet paths continue to reference `css/games/project-starfall.css`.

### 2026-06-12: CSS Loading Partial Extracted

- Created `css/games/project-starfall/loading.css`.
- Moved the loading overlay, transition-loader states, start-screen surface, loading progress bar, and reduced-motion transition override into the new partial.
- Kept the public stylesheet path unchanged by importing the partial from `css/games/project-starfall.css`.
- Updated Starfall CSS contract tests to read the entry stylesheet and recursively inline local `@import url(...)` partials before making assertions.
- Verified with `node --check test.js`, focused Starfall JS syntax checks, `npm run test:starfall:smoke`, `npm run build:css`, and `git diff --check` for the touched files.

- Updated `project-starfall-engine.js` so `changeMap` still owns map validation, runtime rebuilds, player travel placement, analytics, progress events, enemy spawning, and Date.now sampling for newly created dungeon runs while delegating active dungeon/current run mutation to `EngineDungeons`.
- Kept behavior preserving by preserving raw `map.dungeonId` active assignment, existing matching-run preservation, new-run creation only when the current run is missing or has a different raw active dungeon id, respawn status lookup only for new runs, original started/completed timestamp sampling, non-dungeon active/current-run clearing, normalized run dungeon ids, and fresh objective initialization.
- Updated source contracts so general map-change dungeon-state ownership, engine aliasing, and `changeMap` facade delegation remain asserted in both Starfall contract sections.
- Verified with `node --check` for `engine/dungeons.js`, `project-starfall-engine.js`, and `test.js`; a direct helper probe covering dungeon entry, respawning run creation, matching-run preservation, replacement run creation, non-dungeon clearing, raw active id preservation, normalized run ids, and objective initialization; `git diff --check` for tracked touched files plus direct whitespace checks for untracked dungeons/plan files; and `npm run test:starfall:smoke` (1212 checks).
- `npm run test:starfall` was also attempted, but still stops on the known generated map geometry issue in `project-starfall-data.js` (`gearworksVault` slope 11 disconnected left endpoint, reported at `test.js:8144` after this contract-check line shift).

- Updated `project-starfall-data.js` so the compatibility `ProjectStarfallData` bundle consumes `makeTownPlatforms`, `makeTownClimbables`, and `makeDungeonArenaPlatforms` from `DataMapBuilders` while keeping map metadata enrichment, station assignment, shop interior maps, and final `MAPS` assembly in the compatibility bundle.
- Removed stale direct compatibility aliases for `makeClimbableBetweenPlatforms`, `getPlatformDefX`, `getPlatformDefY`, `getPlatformDefW`, and `DUNGEON_ARENA_SKELETONS` after their consumers moved into `data/map-builders.js` or remained in `data/map-layouts.js`.
- No page script order change was needed beyond the existing `data/map-builders.js` load before the compatibility data bundle.
- Updated source contracts so town/dungeon builder ownership, compatibility data-bundle consumption, downstream low-level geometry consumption, and absence of the moved helper declarations from the monolithic compatibility bundle are asserted.
- Verified with `node --check` for `data/map-builders.js`, `project-starfall-data.js`, and `test.js`; a CommonJS town-builder probe covering multiple public town platform/climbable outputs; a CommonJS dungeon-builder probe covering multiple public dungeon platform outputs and missing-skeleton fallback; a browser-style probe proving the compatibility bundle consumed wrapped town and dungeon builder helpers while building maps; and `npm run test:starfall:smoke` (1347 checks).
- `npm run test:starfall` was also attempted, but still stops on the known generated map geometry issue in `project-starfall-data.js` (`gearworksVault` slope 11 disconnected left endpoint, reported at `test.js:9526` with the loop at `test.js:9524` after this contract-check line shift).

### 2026-06-14: UI HUD Timing Constants Moved To HUD Module

- Moved HUD meter animation timing, canvas overlay cache timing, and reward toast presentation/timing constants into `js/games/project-starfall/ui/hud.js`, where the related HUD, overlay-cache, toast, and reward-toast helpers already live.

- Routed quest NPC alignment, map-hunt NPC definition creation, class-trial instance map creation, and performance benchmark map creation through those accessors instead of direct `EngineQuestNpcs.*` and `EngineGeneratedMaps.*` member reads.
- Preserved behavior because each helper still prefers the same module implementation with the same playfield height, `Data`, benchmark map id/name, and benchmark enemy arguments, and the existing inline fallbacks keep the same NPC alignment and generated map behavior when a helper is unavailable.

- Preserved behavior because the helper mirrors the prior `Number(this.shardCraftQuantity || 1) + Number(delta || 0)` expression, including falsey quantity fallback, numeric-string handling, invalid-number propagation into the existing setter normalization, and falsey delta fallback.
- Updated duplicated source contracts so the attunement module is asserted to own shard-craft quantity adjustment, the helper factory exposes it, and the UI facade is asserted to call it.
- Verified with `node --check` for `ui/attunement.js`, `project-starfall-ui.js`, and `test.js`; a focused CommonJS helper probe covering old-formula parity for undefined, zero, string zero, numeric strings, decimals, invalid numbers, booleans, nulls, factory-bound helper output, and facade source lookup; `npm run test:starfall:smoke` (1383 checks); `node test.js --suite=default` (5083 checks); `git diff --check` for the tracked edited files; and explicit trailing-whitespace/tab scans for the untracked plan/module files.
- `npm run test:starfall` was also attempted, but still stops on the known generated map geometry issue in `data/index.js` through the public compatibility data object (`gearworksVault` slope 11 disconnected left endpoint, reported at `test.js:11215` with the loop at `test.js:11213`).

### 2026-06-15: UI Attunement Potential Prompt Reroll Result Ownership Tightened

- Added `getPotentialPromptRerollResultState(...)` to `js/games/project-starfall/ui/attunement.js`, moving regular Attunement reroll result object shaping into the attunement potential helper group.
- Routed the post-engine result assignment in `ProjectStarfallUi.confirmPotentialPrompt(...)` through `getAttunementPotentialHelper('getPotentialPromptRerollResultState')`, with the facade still owning the public method, pre-engine original-potential snapshot, engine call, mutation refresh, prompt-result assignment, queue-refresh timing, and original inline fallback.
- Preserved behavior because the helper mirrors the same regular-result mode, id prefix and uid suffix, item metadata fields, original-potential carry-forward, new-potential clone callback, two `Date.now()` calls around the new-potential clone, no-updated-item prompt uid reset, and clear-result signal used by the prior facade code.
- Updated duplicated source contracts so the attunement module is asserted to own reroll result-state calculation, the helper factory exposes it, and the UI facade is asserted to call it.
- Verified with `node --check` for `ui/attunement.js`, `project-starfall-ui.js`, and `test.js`; a focused CommonJS helper probe covering result flags, id formatting, item metadata, original potential preservation, new-potential cloning, timestamp/clone call ordering, missing-updated-item clear state, factory-bound helper output, and facade source lookup; `npm run test:starfall:smoke` (1383 checks); `node test.js --suite=default` (5083 checks); `git diff --check` for the tracked edited files; and explicit trailing-whitespace/tab scans for the untracked plan/module files.
- `npm run test:starfall` was also attempted, but still stops on the known generated map geometry issue in `data/index.js` through the public compatibility data object (`gearworksVault` slope 11 disconnected left endpoint, reported at `test.js:11218` with the loop at `test.js:11216`).

### 2026-06-16: Practical Milestone Completed - Starfall Runtime Suite Restored

- Restored the full Starfall runtime suite to green while maintaining behavior-compatible organization work: `npm run test:starfall` now passes 27,691 checks with the `starfall-runtime` suite.
- Closed the generated map geometry blocker by preserving connected dungeon arena traversal, normalizing party-tier lane spacing, adding transition-slope generation, and narrowing optional supplemental ledges so static and runtime platform contracts agree.
- Kept the combat HUD readable after module extraction by compacting combat metric row spacing and matching the extracted HUD helper and UI fallback geometry.
- Preserved pet potion drag/drop behavior by carrying bind-drop completion metadata through both helper-dispatched and fallback pet-slot assignment paths.
- Preserved companion navigation by allowing direct routed jump/drop links to fire before ground snapping blocks a valid transition.
- Split inventory normalization from admin restore behavior: fresh starter states no longer receive the admin console, while legacy/restored saves still regain admin tooling when appropriate.
- Reconciled test/static contracts with the extracted modules for inventory items, inventory config, hover routing, keybindings, settings, HUD, and action icon layout without weakening the behavior assertions.
- Suppressed attunement progress toasts for `noEmit` batched progress events while preserving reward state updates, rare potential results, and cube-fragment rewards.

Next finite boundary:

- The next phase should touch one module cluster only. Stop after extracting or cleaning a single UI or engine helper cluster, update the matching static references, and keep `npm run test:starfall` green.
- Do not begin another cluster in the same goal turn, even if the first one passes quickly.
- Treat any failing full-suite contract as a stop condition for that slice; fix the regression or roll the slice forward narrowly before continuing.

### 2026-06-16: Engine Inventory Admin Console Helper Ownership Tightened

- Added `ensureAdminConsoleItem(state, options)` to `js/games/project-starfall/engine/inventory.js`, moving restored-save admin console consumable repair into the engine inventory helper module.
- Routed the `project-starfall-engine.js` facade through `getEngineInventoryHelper('ensureAdminConsoleItem')`, while preserving the existing inline fallback, `Data` dependency, and `ADMIN_CONSOLE_ITEM_ID` behavior.
- Preserved the practical milestone boundary by touching one helper cluster only and leaving starter-state behavior unchanged: fresh starter states do not receive the admin console, while restored/legacy saves still regain admin tooling when the consumable definition exists.
- Updated duplicated static contracts so the inventory module is asserted to own admin console item repair and the engine facade is asserted to route through the inventory helper.
- Verified with `node --check` for `engine/inventory.js`, `project-starfall-engine.js`, and `test.js`; a focused CommonJS helper probe covering admin console restore and missing-definition no-op behavior; and `npm run test:starfall` (27,691 checks).

Next finite boundary:

- The next phase should again touch one module cluster only. Stop after extracting or cleaning a single UI or engine helper cluster, update the matching static references, and keep `npm run test:starfall` green.
- Do not begin another cluster in the same goal turn.

## Success Definition

This organization work is successful when:

- New contributors can identify where to add a skill, item, map, panel, or runtime system without searching huge files.
- The engine mostly coordinates systems instead of owning every system directly.
- The UI mostly coordinates lifecycle/input/windows instead of owning every panel implementation directly.
- Static data can be edited by domain.
- CSS changes are localized by UI surface.
- Runtime behavior, route behavior, save compatibility, and visual output remain identical throughout the refactor.
